sections.heading: What is Java?
sections.text: Java is a high-level, class-based, object-oriented programming language used to build secure and robust applications.

sections.heading: Why is Java Popular?
items: Platform Independent – Write Once, Run Anywhere

sections.heading: JVM (Java Virtual Machine)
sections.text: Executes bytecode, manages memory, performs security checks.

sections.heading: JRE (Java Runtime Environment)
sections.text: JVM + Libraries required to run Java programs.

sections.heading: JDK (Java Development Kit)
sections.text: JRE + Compiler + Debugger + Developer tools.

sections.heading: Java Execution Process
sections.text: Source (.java) → Compiler → Bytecode (.class) → JVM

sections.heading: Hello World Program

Java.title: How to Install Java (Windows / Linux / macOS)

sections.heading: Install Java on Windows
steps: Visit official Oracle website and download JDK.
Add JDK bin folder to PATH in Environment Variables.

sections.text: A variable is a name given to a memory location that stores data. It acts as a container whose value can be changed during execution. Every variable in Java must have a datatype.

items: Every variable has a memory address.
Datatype decides how much memory the variable will take.
The value can change, but datatype cannot change.
Instance Variable — Declared inside class but outside methods.
Static Variable — Declared with static keyword. Shared among all objects.

sections.text: dataType variableName = value;

21
Bhumika
Data types define the type and size of data stored in a variable. Java has two categories of data types: Primitive and Non-Primitive.

items: float — 4 bytes, decimal values end with f

sections.text: Non-primitive data types are created by programmers. They store multiple values or complex structures.

items: Wrapper Classes — Integer, Double, Boolean etc.

Type casting means converting one data type into another. Java supports two types: Implicit (Automatic) and Explicit (Manual).
Converting smaller datatype into larger datatype automatically. Example: int → double.
Converting larger datatype into smaller datatype manually. Example: double → int.
Operators are symbols that perform operations on variables and values. Java provides different types of operators to manipulate data and variables.
Arithmetic operators are used to perform basic mathematical operations.

sections.code:
int a = 10;
int b = 5;

System.out.println(a + b); // Addition
System.out.println(a - b); // Subtraction
System.out.println(a * b); // Multiplication
System.out.println(a / b); // Division
System.out.println(a % b); // Modulus

sections.text: Unary operators operate on a single operand to perform operations like increment, decrement, or negation.

sections.code:
int x = 10;
System.out.println(++x); // Pre-increment
System.out.println(x++); // Post-increment
System.out.println(--x); // Pre-decrement
System.out.println(x--); // Post-decrement
System.out.println(-x); // Unary minus
System.out.println(!true); // Logical NOT

sections.text: Relational operators are used to compare two values and return a boolean result (true/false).

false
true
false
true
false
true
Logical operators are used to combine multiple boolean expressions.

sections.code:
boolean p = true, q = false;
System.out.println(p && q); // Logical AND
System.out.println(p || q); // Logical OR
System.out.println(!p);      // Logical NOT

sections.text:
false
true
false
Bitwise operators operate on bits of integer types to perform AND, OR, XOR, NOT, and shift operations.

sections.code:
int a = 5; // 0101
int b = 3; // 0011
System.out.println(a & b); // AND
System.out.println(a | b); // OR
System.out.println(a ^ b); // XOR
System.out.println(~a);    // NOT
System.out.println(a << 1); // Left shift
System.out.println(a >> 1); // Right shift

sections.text: Assignment operators are used to assign values to variables with optional arithmetic operations.

sections.code:
int a = 10;
a += 5; // a = a + 5
a -= 3; // a = a - 3
a *= 2; // a = a * 2
a /= 4; // a = a / 4
a %= 3; // a = a % 3
System.out.println(a);

sections.text: The ternary operator ( ? : ) is a shortcut for an if-else statement.
An expression is a combination of variables, operators, and values that evaluates to a single value.

Sum: 15

Loops are used to execute a block of code multiple times until a specified condition is false. They help reduce code repetition.

items: For Loop — Executes code for a specific number of times.
While Loop — Executes code while a condition is true.
Do-While Loop — Executes code at least once and then repeats while condition is true.
Enhanced For Loop (for-each) — Iterates over arrays or collections.

sections.text: The for loop repeats a block of code a fixed number of times based on the initialization, condition, and increment/decrement.

Iteration: 1

The while loop executes a block of code repeatedly as long as the given condition is true.

Iteration: 1

The do-while loop executes the code block at least once and then checks the condition for further iterations.

Iteration: 1

The enhanced for loop iterates over arrays or collections without using an index.
A nested loop is a loop inside another loop. The inner loop completes all its iterations for each iteration of the outer loop.

items: continue — Skips the current iteration and continues with the next.
return — Exits from the current method (also stops loops inside).

A function (or method) is a block of code that performs a specific task. Methods help in code reusability and modular programming.

Method overloading means having multiple methods in the same class with the same name but different parameters (different number or type).

items: Methods must have the same name.
Parameters must differ in number or type.
Return type alone cannot overload a method.
Overloading improves code readability and usability.

An array is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type. Arrays help in storing multiple values in a single variable.

items: Arrays store multiple values of the same type in a single variable.
Length of an array can be accessed using arrayName.length.
Multidimensional arrays are arrays of arrays.
Array size is fixed after creation.

StringBuilder is a mutable sequence of characters. Unlike Strings, it can be modified after creation and is more efficient for repeated modifications.
StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("Hello");
Hi Java World

items: Strings are immutable, StringBuilder is mutable.
String modifications create new objects; StringBuilder modifies the same object.
StringBuilder is faster for repeated modifications.
Both classes have many useful methods for handling text.

Java.title: OOP Basics — Class & Object in Java

sections.text: OOP is a programming paradigm based on the concept of 'objects', which contain data (attributes) and methods (functions). Java is an object-oriented language.
A class is a blueprint or template for creating objects. It defines the properties (variables) and behaviors (methods) of objects.
An object is an instance of a class. Each object has its own copy of attributes defined in the class and can use the methods of the class.

Name: Bhumika
Age: 21

Brand: BMW, Speed: 150
Brand: Audi, Speed: 180

items: Class is a blueprint; object is an instance of a class.
Objects store data in attributes and perform actions using methods.
Multiple objects can be created from the same class.
Classes improve modularity and reusability of code.

sections.heading: 8. Full Example — Class with Multiple Methods

sections.heading: 6. Key Points of OOP Deep Concepts

items: Encapsulation hides data and provides controlled access.
Inheritance allows code reuse from parent class.
Polymorphism allows objects to take multiple forms.
Abstraction hides implementation and shows functionality.

sections.text: A constructor is a special method used to initialize objects. It has the same name as the class and no return type.
Name: Bhumika, Roll No: 101
Constructor overloading means having multiple constructors in a class with different parameter lists.

The 'this' keyword refers to the current object of the class. It is used to access instance variables or call constructors.
Name: Bhumika, Roll No: 101
A constructor can call another constructor of the same class using 'this()'. This is useful for constructor chaining.

items: Constructors initialize objects and can be overloaded.
'this' refers to current object and can call another constructor.
'super' refers to parent class and is used to access parent members or constructors.
Use 'this' and 'super' carefully to avoid confusion.

sections.text: Inheritance is an OOP concept where a class (child/subclass) acquires the properties and methods of another class (parent/superclass). It promotes code reusability.

Vehicle is starting
Car is driving

items: Single Inheritance – Child inherits from one parent.
Multilevel Inheritance – Chain of inheritance (A → B → C).
Hierarchical Inheritance – Multiple classes inherit from one parent.
Java does not support multiple inheritance with classes, but interfaces can achieve it.

sections.text: Polymorphism means 'many forms'. An object can take multiple forms. In Java, there are two types: Compile-time polymorphism (method overloading) and Run-time polymorphism (method overriding).
Method overriding occurs when a subclass provides its own implementation of a method already defined in its parent class.
Dog barks
The 'super' keyword allows a subclass to access parent class variables, methods, or constructors.

Car is starting
Vehicle is starting

items: Inheritance allows code reuse and forms parent-child relationships.
Polymorphism allows objects to take many forms.
Use 'super' to access parent class members in child class.

sections.text: Abstraction is hiding implementation details and showing only functionality. Achieved using abstract classes or interfaces.
Drawing Circle
Drawing Rectangle

items: Abstraction hides implementation and shows only functionality.
Abstract class can have both abstract and non-abstract methods.
Interface can only have abstract methods (Java 8+ supports default and static methods).
A class can implement multiple interfaces but extend only one class.

sections.text: A package in Java is a namespace that groups related classes and interfaces together. It helps in organizing large projects, avoiding class name conflicts, and providing access control.
👉 Real-life Example: Just like folders in your computer organize files, Java packages organize classes.

👉 Types of Packages:
1. Built-in Packages – Example: java.util, java.lang
2. User-defined Packages – Created by programmers
Hello from package!
Access modifiers define the visibility (scope) of variables, methods, and classes.

items: private — only inside the same class
default — only inside the same package
protected → same class + package + child class

sections.text: 👉 Memory Trick: private < default < protected < public
The Java Collections Framework (JCF) is a unified architecture for storing and manipulating groups of objects. It provides interfaces, implementations, and algorithms to handle data efficiently.

👉 It replaces traditional arrays with dynamic, flexible, and powerful data structures.
👉 It is part of java.util package.
👉 It provides both data storage and data manipulation methods.
Collection is the root interface. It has three main sub-interfaces:

items: Set → Unordered, does not allow duplicates
Queue → Follows FIFO (First In First Out)

sections.text: 👉 Map is NOT part of Collection interface but part of the framework.

items: LinkedList → Doubly linked list, fast insertion/deletion
HashMap → Key-value pair, no duplicate keys

sections.text:
👉 ArrayList uses a dynamic array internally.
👉 HashMap uses hashing (hashCode + equals).
👉 LinkedList uses nodes with pointers.
👉 TreeSet/TreeMap use Red-Black Tree (self-balancing BST).
Generics in Java allow you to create classes, interfaces, and methods with a parameterized type, ensuring type safety at compile time.

👉 Introduced in Java 5
👉 Avoids ClassCastException
👉 Improves code readability
Without generics, collections can store any type of object, leading to runtime errors.
An exception is an unwanted or unexpected event that occurs during the execution of a program and disrupts the normal flow of instructions.

👉 Example: Dividing a number by zero, accessing invalid array index, file not found.
👉 If not handled, program will terminate abnormally.

👉 Root class: Throwable
👉 Two main types:
1. Error (serious problems, not handled)
2. Exception (can be handled)

Throwable
  ├── Error (OutOfMemoryError, StackOverflowError)
  └── Exception
        ├── Checked Exception
        └── Unchecked Exception

items: Checked Exception → Checked at compile time (IOException, SQLException)
Unchecked Exception → Occurs at runtime (ArithmeticException, NullPointerException)

sections.text: try block contains risky code and catch block handles the exception.
Cannot divide by zero
finally block always executes whether exception occurs or not.
👉 You can handle multiple exceptions using multiple catch blocks.
throw keyword is used to explicitly throw an exception.
throws keyword is used to declare exceptions in method signature.
User-defined exceptions created by extending Exception class.

👉 try → risky code
👉 catch → handle error
👉 finally → always run
👉 throw → create error
👉 throws → declare error
File handling in Java is used to create, read, write, and delete files. It allows programs to store data permanently in storage devices.

👉 Java provides java.io and java.nio packages for file handling.
👉 Data stored in files remains even after program execution ends.
File class is used to create and manage files and directories.
👉 java.nio.file package provides faster and modern file handling.
👉 Create → Write → Read → Close
Multithreading is a process of executing multiple threads simultaneously to improve performance and CPU utilization.

👉 Thread is a lightweight process.
👉 Used in games, animations, background tasks, etc.
Synchronization is used to control access to shared resources to avoid data inconsistency.
